name: dumbbell incline alternate press
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Once at shoulder height, rotate your wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.
Push the dumbbells up with your chest and shoulders, extending your arms fully.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs. Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward. Once at shoulder height, rotate your wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward. Push the dumbbells up with your chest and shoulders, extending your arms fully. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
